golden_hour: The first 1-2 hours after a venomous bite are critical. Antivenom given early prevents death and disability. Do NOT waste time on ojha/kobiraj (traditional healers) — go straight to the nearest hospital or Upazila Health Complex that stocks polyvalent antivenom.
summary: Envenomation from venomous snakes (cobra, krait, Russell's viper, green pit viper). Bangladesh reports around 7,000 snakebite deaths per year, mostly rural, often because patients visit traditional healers first. Russell's viper (chandrabora) has re-emerged as a major killer in northwest and central districts.
symptoms: Fang marks, local pain and swelling
Drooping eyelids and blurred vision (neurotoxic - cobra/krait)
Bleeding from gums, blood in urine (hemotoxic - Russell's viper)
causes: Bite by venomous snake - cobra (gokhra), krait (kalkeuti), Russell's viper (chandrabora), green pit viper (sobuj bora)
risk_factors: Farming and walking barefoot at night
Sleeping on the floor in rural homes (krait bites sleepers)
Handling or trying to kill snakes
red_flags: Drooping eyelids, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing
treatment_overview: Emergency: keep the patient calm and still, immobilize the bitten limb at heart level, remove tight items (rings, bangles). Do NOT cut, suck, apply tourniquet, or use ice. Transport immediately. Definitive treatment is polyvalent antivenom in hospital plus airway and ventilation support, and neostigmine for neurotoxic bites. Kidney support may be needed for viper bites.
prevention: Use a torch and wear shoes when walking at night
Sleep on a raised bed under a tucked mosquito net
Keep grass cut and clear rubbish around the home
Do not put hands into holes or under logs
Know the nearest antivenom-stocking hospital
bd_context: National antivenom is stocked at district hospitals and many Upazila Health Complexes. Reaching antivenom within 1-2 hours saves lives. The biggest preventable cause of death is delay from visiting ojha/kobiraj first. Venom Research Centre and DMCH provide referral support.

qa.q: Sape kamor dile prothome ki korbo?
qa.a: Rogi ke shanto rakhun, kamranো hat/pa nordochora bondho korun ebong hridpinder somaan uchutay rakhun. Angti/churi khule felun. Kono vabei kata, chusa, githa (tourniquet) ba boróf debén na. Sathe sathe nikotostho hospital e niye jan jekhane antivenom ache. Ojha/kobiraj er kache somoy noshto korben na - eta e mrityur boro karon.

qa.q: Which hospitals in Bangladesh have antivenom?
qa.a: Polyvalent antivenom is stocked at district (Sadar) hospitals and many Upazila Health Complexes, plus large centres like Dhaka Medical College Hospital. Call ahead if possible, but do not delay - go to the nearest government hospital that treats snakebite.

qa.q: Is every snakebite dangerous?
qa.a: No - many bites are from non-venomous snakes or are dry bites with no venom. But you cannot tell reliably at home, and Russell's viper and krait bites can kill. Treat every bite as an emergency and go to hospital for observation for at least 24 hours.

qa.q: Why should I avoid a tourniquet for snakebite?
qa.a: Tight tourniquets can cut off blood flow, worsen local tissue damage, cause gangrene, and release a surge of venom when removed. The modern method is pressure immobilization for neurotoxic bites and simply keeping the limb still - not a tight band.

qa.q: Krait (kalkeuti) kamor er lokkhon ki?
qa.a: Krait proyshoi ratre ghumonto manush ke kamray ebong betha kom hoy, tai onek shomoy bujha jay na. Sokale uthe chokher pata jhule pora, dhok gilte kosto, sash niye kosto, pet betha - eguli neurotoxic biser lokkhon. Sathe sathe hospital e jan.

qa.q: How long should I stay in hospital after a snakebite?
qa.a: At least 24 hours of observation even if you feel fine, because krait and some viper venom effects can be delayed. Repeated blood clotting tests (20-minute whole blood clotting test) and neurological checks guide treatment.

qa.q: Can I drive myself after a snakebite?
qa.a: No. Venom can cause sudden paralysis, drowsiness, or shock. Have someone else take you, keep still, and keep the bitten limb immobilized during transport.

qa.q: Russell's viper (chandrabora) kamor keno beshi bipojjonok?
qa.a: Chandrabora bish rokto joma badha noshto kore, ফলে masuri diye rokto pora, prosrabe rokto, ebong kidney bikol hote pare. Ei bishe druto antivenom ebong kidney support dorkar hote pare. Rajshahi, Rajbari, Chapainawabganj o char elakay eta abar bere geche.

qa.q: Sap kamranor pore ki khabo?
qa.a: Kichu khaওয়ার cheye druto hospital e jaওয়া joruri. Rogi conscious thakle olpo pani dite paren kintu bomi hole ba osusto hole mukhe kichu deben na. Onno kono ghoroa treatment cheshta korben na.

qa.q: Does killing the snake help doctors treat me?
qa.a: Do not risk another bite trying to catch or kill it. A phone photo from a safe distance can help identify the species, but antivenom in Bangladesh is polyvalent and works for the common venomous snakes regardless.

golden_hour: Time is muscle. The golden window is the first 1-2 hours from chest pain onset. Getting to a hospital with a cath lab or clot-dissolving treatment (thrombolysis) within this window can save heart muscle and life. Chew one aspirin (300 mg) unless allergic, and arrange transport immediately.
summary: Acute myocardial infarction - a coronary artery blocked by a clot, starving heart muscle of oxygen. A leading cause of death in Bangladesh, increasingly in people under 50 due to smoking, diabetes, and hypertension. Early recognition and rapid transport are life-saving.
symptoms: Central chest pain or heavy pressure, often on the left side
Pain spreading to left arm, jaw, neck, or back
Sudden extreme weakness or a sense of doom
In women, elderly, and diabetics may present as only breathlessness, fatigue, or indigestion-like discomfort
causes: Rupture of atherosclerotic plaque causing a coronary artery clot
risk_factors: Family history of early heart disease
red_flags: Chest pain lasting more than 15-20 minutes
Chest pain with sweating, vomiting, or breathlessness
Left arm or jaw pain with cold sweat
treatment_overview: Emergency: arrange help, have the patient rest, chew 300 mg aspirin (unless allergic or bleeding). Go to the nearest cardiac-capable hospital immediately. Definitive treatment is primary angioplasty (PCI/stent) or thrombolysis, plus dual antiplatelets, statins, and beta-blockers. Do not wait to see if it passes.
prevention: Stop all tobacco
Control blood pressure, sugar, and cholesterol
Eat less oil, salt, and red meat; more vegetables and fish
30 minutes of walking most days
Manage stress and sleep
bd_context: Cardiac hospitals include National Institute of Cardiovascular Diseases (NICVD), Ibrahim Cardiac, and cath labs in major private hospitals. Many deaths happen at home from delay. Any adult with chest pain plus sweating should reach an emergency department within the hour.

qa.q: Buker bampashe betha, ki korbo?
qa.a: Jodi buker majhkhane ba bampashe chapa dhoroner betha 15-20 minute er beshi thake, sathe thanda gham, bomi bhab, ba sash koshto thake - eta heart attack hote pare. Rogi ke boshiye bishram diye, aspirin (300mg) chibiye khaওয়ান (jodi allergy na thake), ebong sathe sathe hospital e niye jan. Nijer gari cholaben na.

qa.q: How do I tell a heart attack from gastric pain?
qa.a: It can be hard. Gastric/acidity pain is usually burning, related to food, and eases with antacids. Heart attack pain is more of a heavy pressure, may spread to the arm/jaw, and comes with sweating, nausea, or breathlessness. When in doubt, especially with diabetes, hypertension, or smoking, treat it as a heart problem and go to hospital.

qa.q: Heart attack e aspirin keno khete hoy?
qa.a: Aspirin rokto joma badhte badha dey, ফলে dhomonir clot boro howa komay. Ekta 300mg aspirin chibiye khele upokar hoy - tobe jodi aspirin e allergy thake ba rokto porar shomossa thake tahole noy. Ei tuku kore i hospital e jan.

qa.q: Can young people have heart attacks in Bangladesh?
qa.a: Yes, increasingly. Smoking, tobacco (jorda/gul), diabetes, and high cholesterol are causing heart attacks in people in their 30s and 40s. Chest pain should never be dismissed as too young for a heart attack.

qa.q: Meyeder heart attack er lokkhon ki alada?
qa.a: Ha, meyeder, boyoskoder ebong diabetic der khetre buke tibro betha na o thakte pare. Onek shomoy sudhu haf dhora, tumul klanti, ba bodhojomer moto onubhuti hoy. Ei rokom hole o obohela na kore doctor dekhan.

qa.q: What is the golden hour for a heart attack?
qa.a: The first 1-2 hours from symptom onset. Restoring blood flow within this window - by stent (angioplasty) or clot-dissolving injection - saves the most heart muscle. Every minute of delay means more permanent damage.

qa.q: Heart attack er por kotodin bishram lagbe?
qa.a: Doctor er poramorsho onujayi sadharonoto koyek soptaho. Cardiac rehabilitation, oshudh niyomito khaওয়া, dhumpan bondho, ebong dhire dhire halka byam shuru kora hoy. Nijei sidhanto neben na.

qa.q: Which hospital should I go to for a heart attack in Dhaka?
qa.a: Go to the nearest hospital with an emergency department and, ideally, a cardiac cath lab - such as NICVD, Ibrahim Cardiac, or a large hospital with 24-hour cardiology. Speed matters more than choosing the best hospital far away.

qa.q: Ki khele heart attack er ঝুঁki komey?
qa.a: Tel, lobon o lal mangsho kom khan; beshi shobji, mach, o phol khan. Dhumpan o tamak sompurno bondho korun. Diabetes, presar o cholesterol niyontrone rakhun ebong protidin hatun.

qa.q: Is chest pain always a heart attack?
qa.a: No. Chest pain can be from acidity, muscle strain, anxiety, or lung problems. But because a missed heart attack can be fatal, any chest pain with sweating, breathlessness, or spread to the arm/jaw should be checked urgently - especially with heart risk factors.

golden_hour: For clot-type (ischemic) stroke, clot-dissolving treatment works only within about 4.5 hours of symptom onset, and clot retrieval within a few more hours. The earlier, the better the recovery. Note the exact time symptoms started and reach a stroke-ready hospital immediately.
summary: Stroke is sudden loss of brain function from a blocked (ischemic) or burst (hemorrhagic) blood vessel. A top cause of death and long-term disability in Bangladesh. Rapid recognition with FAST and urgent hospital transport dramatically improve outcomes.
symptoms: Arm or leg weakness on one side
Slurred or jumbled speech, or unable to speak
Sudden loss of balance or vision
causes: Rupture of a brain vessel (hemorrhagic), often from uncontrolled high blood pressure
treatment_overview: Emergency: note the time symptoms started, keep the patient lying safely, do NOT give food or water (swallowing may be impaired), and go to a hospital with CT scan and stroke care at once. Treatment depends on type: thrombolysis/clot removal for ischemic, blood pressure control and sometimes surgery for hemorrhagic. Never give aspirin before a CT confirms the type.
prevention: Control blood pressure strictly
Manage diabetes and cholesterol
Stop smoking
Treat irregular heartbeat
Regular exercise and low-salt diet
bd_context: The National Institute of Neurosciences (NINS) in Dhaka and major medical college hospitals provide stroke care and CT scanning. Because many patients arrive late, most miss the thrombolysis window - public awareness of FAST is critical.

qa.q: Stroke er lokkhon kivabe chinbo?
qa.a: FAST mone rakhun - F (Face): mukher ek pash jhule pora; A (Arm): ek hat/pa durbol hoye jaওয়া; S (Speech): kotha jorano ba bolte na para; T (Time): eguli hotath dekha dile shomoy noshto na kore hospital e jan. Symptom shuru howar somoy khyal rakhun.

qa.q: Why should I not give food or water during a stroke?
qa.a: Stroke can paralyze the swallowing muscles, so food or water may go into the lungs and cause dangerous pneumonia or choking. Keep the person comfortable and get to hospital - feeding can wait for medical assessment.

qa.q: Stroke e ki aspirin dewa jabe?
qa.a: Na, CT scan e stroke er dhoron (clot na rokto khoron) na jana porjonto aspirin deওয়া bipojjonok. Rokto khoroner stroke hole aspirin obostha aro kharap kore. Tai age hospital e CT scan korun.

qa.q: How much time do I have to treat a stroke?
qa.a: For a clot-type (ischemic) stroke, the clot-dissolving injection works best within 4.5 hours of onset, and clot removal within about 6-24 hours in selected cases. Sooner is always better - reach a CT-equipped hospital immediately.

qa.q: Presar beshi thakle stroke er ঝুঁki ki bere jay?
qa.a: Ha, oniyontrito ucchu roktochap i stroke er sobcheye boro karon, bishesh kore rokto khoroner stroke er. Niyomito presar mepe oshudh khele ei ঝুঁki onek komey.

qa.q: Can a stroke be prevented?
qa.a: Largely yes. Controlling blood pressure, diabetes, and cholesterol, stopping smoking, treating irregular heartbeat, exercising, and eating low-salt food prevent most strokes. If you had a TIA (mini-stroke), treatment prevents a major one.

qa.q: Mini stroke (TIA) mane ki?
qa.a: TIA holo kichukhoner jonno stroke er lokkhon ja nijei theke jay. Kintu eta boro stroke er warning - obohela korben na. Druto doctor dekhiye oshudh o test korle boro stroke thekano jay.

qa.q: Which hospital treats stroke in Dhaka?
qa.a: The National Institute of Neurosciences (NINS) and large medical college hospitals with 24-hour CT scanning and neurology. Go to the nearest such hospital - do not travel far and lose the treatment window.

qa.q: Stroke er por ki puropuri sustho howa jay?
qa.a: Onek khetre physiotherapy o niyomito treatment e onek ta sere otha jay, bishesh kore jodi druto treatment paওয়া jay. Kotota sarbe ta depend kore stroke koto boro chilo ebong koto druto treatment shuru holo tar upor.

qa.q: Does a headache always mean stroke?
qa.a: No, most headaches are not strokes. But a sudden worst-ever thunderclap headache, especially with vomiting, neck stiffness, or one-sided weakness, can be a bleeding stroke and needs emergency assessment.

regions: much of southern and central Bangladesh
golden_hour: Not an acute emergency, but the golden action is early switching to an arsenic-safe water source. Skin changes are reversible in early stages if arsenic exposure stops; late-stage cancers are not.
summary: Chronic poisoning from drinking tubewell water contaminated with naturally occurring arsenic. Bangladesh's arsenic contamination is one of the largest mass poisonings in history, affecting tens of millions. Long exposure causes characteristic skin changes and raises cancer risk.
symptoms: Dark spots on skin (hyperpigmentation), especially chest and back
Hardened, thickened skin on palms and soles (keratosis)
White spots on darkened skin (raindrop pattern)
causes: Long-term drinking of arsenic-contaminated groundwater from shallow tubewells
risk_factors: Living in a district with high-arsenic groundwater
Drinking from an untested or red-marked tubewell for years
red_flags: A skin sore or spot that grows, ulcerates, or bleeds
treatment_overview: The core treatment is stopping exposure: switch to a tested arsenic-safe source (deep tubewell, treated water, rainwater). Nutritional support with vitamins and protein helps. Skin lesions are monitored for cancer; suspicious lesions need biopsy. There is no chelation cure for chronic low-level exposure.
prevention: Test your tubewell - use only green-marked (safe) sources
Use deep tubewells, piped supply, or arsenic filters
Improve nutrition (protein, vegetables, vitamins A, C, E)
Community water testing programs
bd_context: DPHE and NGOs have painted tubewells green (safe) or red (unsafe). Millions still drink from red or untested wells. Affected districts in central and southern Bangladesh have specialized arsenicosis clinics. Early lesions can regress once the water source changes.

qa.q: Nolkup er panite arsenic ache ki kore bujhbo?
qa.a: Kheye ba dekhe bujha jay na - pani porishkar o gondhohin thaklei o arsenic thakte pare. DPHE ba local NGO diye tubewell test korান. Nirapod tubewell shobuj rong e ebong bipojjonok tubewell lal rong e mark kora thake. Sudhu shobuj tubewell er pani khan.

qa.q: What are the first signs of arsenicosis?
qa.a: Early signs are dark spots on the skin (especially chest, back, and limbs) and thickening of the palms and soles. White raindrop spots on darkened skin are characteristic. These early changes can improve if you stop drinking arsenic water.

qa.q: Arsenic er dag ki sere jay?
qa.a: Suru dike, jodi arsenic pani khaওয়া bondho kora hoy ebong pushtikor khabar khaওয়া hoy, tobe chamrar dag onek ta kome jete pare. Kintu deri hole ba cancer hoye gele ta ar theke jay - tai age i pani source poriborton koron.

qa.q: Can arsenic in water cause cancer?
qa.a: Yes. Long-term arsenic exposure raises the risk of skin, bladder, kidney, and lung cancer. This is why any skin sore that grows, ulcerates, or does not heal in an arsenic-affected area must be checked and biopsied.

qa.q: Arsenic filter ki kaj kore?
qa.a: Ha, valo mander arsenic filter (jemon SONO filter ba certified household filter) pani theke arsenic onek ta komay. Tobe filter niyomito porishkar o maintain korte hobe. Gobhir (deep) tubewell ba treated piped water aro nirapod bikolpo.

qa.q: Which areas of Bangladesh have the most arsenic?
qa.a: Central and southern districts such as Comilla, Chandpur, Noakhali, Munshiganj, Faridpur, Satkhira, and Jashore have high groundwater arsenic. But contamination is patchy - even neighbouring wells can differ, so individual testing matters.

qa.q: Arsenicosis er cikitsha ki?
qa.a: Mul cikitsha holo arsenic pani sompurno bondho kora ebong nirapod pani khaওয়া. Sathe valo pushti (protein, shobji, vitamin) shorir ke shohay kore. Chamrar khoti niyomito monitor kora hoy ebong sondehojonok kichu hole biopsy kora hoy.

qa.q: Is arsenicosis contagious?
qa.a: No. It is not infectious and cannot spread from person to person. It comes only from long-term exposure to arsenic-contaminated water, so a whole family using the same well may be affected together.

qa.q: Rannar panite o ki arsenic somossa?
qa.a: Ha, ranna o pan - dui khetrei nirapod pani bebohar korা uchit, karon randhle o arsenic theke jay. Tobe golao/washing er cheye khaওয়া o rannar pani niye beshi sotorko hote hobe.

qa.q: Where can I get treated for arsenicosis?
qa.a: Affected districts have arsenicosis clinics linked to Upazila Health Complexes and DGHS programs. A general physician or dermatologist can assess skin lesions, and suspicious ones are referred for biopsy and cancer screening.

regions: the Nipah belt of western and central Bangladesh
golden_hour: There is no specific cure, so prevention is everything. In an outbreak or after drinking raw date-palm sap, any fever with confusion or breathing difficulty needs immediate hospital care and isolation.
summary: A deadly virus spread mainly by drinking raw date-palm sap (kacha khejur er rosh) contaminated by fruit-bat saliva, and by close contact with infected people. Bangladesh has near-annual winter outbreaks with very high fatality (~70%). Encephalitis (brain inflammation) is the main danger.
causes: Nipah virus from fruit bats, transmitted by raw date-palm sap, contaminated fruit, or close contact with an infected patient's secretions
risk_factors: Drinking raw date-palm sap in winter
Eating fruit partially bitten by bats
Caring for a Nipah patient without protection
Living in the western/central Nipah belt during December-March
red_flags: Any fever after drinking raw date-palm sap in an outbreak area
treatment_overview: No specific antiviral is proven. Care is supportive in hospital with isolation: managing seizures, breathing support, and fluids. Because it spreads person-to-person, strict infection control and barrier nursing are essential.
prevention: Do NOT drink raw date-palm sap (kacha khejur er rosh) - boil it or avoid it
Do not eat fruit that may be bat-bitten
Wash fruit well
Use gloves/mask and hand hygiene when caring for patients
Follow outbreak advisories from IEDCR
bd_context: IEDCR investigates yearly Nipah outbreaks, mostly in the western districts, peaking in winter when date-palm sap is collected. Awareness campaigns urge against drinking raw rosh. Skin-covered (bana) sap collection reduces bat contamination.

qa.q: Kacha khejur er rosh khaওয়া ki nirapod?
qa.a: Na, sitkale kacha khejur er rosh khaওয়া Nipah virus er sobcheye boro karon - badur er lala theke rosh dushito hoy. Rosh futiye (jal diye) khele virus more jay. Kacha rosh puropuri эড়িয়ে chola i nirapod.

qa.q: What are the danger signs of Nipah virus?
qa.a: Fever with drowsiness, confusion, seizures, or difficulty breathing - especially after drinking raw date-palm sap in winter in an affected district. These signal brain involvement (encephalitis) and need immediate hospital care and isolation.

qa.q: Nipah ki manush theke manushe chorায়?
qa.a: Ha, ghonishtho jogajoge - bishesh kore rogir lala, kashi ba secretion theke - Nipah chorate pare. Tai rogi ke isolation e rakha ebong porichorjar somoy mask o glove pora joruri.

qa.q: Is there a cure for Nipah virus?
qa.a: There is no proven specific antiviral cure. Treatment is supportive in hospital - controlling seizures, supporting breathing, and preventing spread. Because fatality is high, prevention (avoiding raw sap and using protection) is the real answer.

qa.q: Nipah outbreak kon somoy hoy?
qa.a: Sadharonoto sitkale (December theke March) jokhon khejur er rosh songroho kora hoy. Poshchim o moddho Bangladesh er jelaguli - Faridpur, Rajbari, Kushtia, Meherpur - e beshi dekha jay.

qa.q: Can I eat dates or date fruit safely?
qa.a: The risk is from raw sap and from fruit possibly bitten by bats, not from properly washed, intact fruit. Wash all fruit well, discard any with bite marks, and avoid raw palm sap during outbreak season.

qa.q: Rosh jal dile ki virus more?
qa.a: Ha, rosh valo kore futiye (jal diye) nile Nipah virus noshto hoy. Sudhu tajā/kacha rosh e i bipod. Tobuo outbreak er somoy sotorko thakai valo.

qa.q: How deadly is Nipah virus?
qa.a: Very - case fatality in Bangladesh outbreaks is around 70%, among the highest of any virus. Survivors may have lasting neurological problems. This is why avoiding raw date-palm sap in winter is so strongly advised.

qa.q: Nipah rogi ke barite rakha jabe?
qa.a: Na, sondeho hole hospital e niye jan jekhane isolation o supportive care ache. Barite rakhle poribarer onno sodossoder o soংক্রमণ er ঝুঁki thake. IEDCR ke janানো o infection control mena joruri.

qa.q: Who should I contact in a Nipah outbreak?
qa.a: Contact the nearest Upazila Health Complex or district hospital, and outbreaks are investigated by IEDCR. Report clusters of fever-with-encephalitis quickly, and follow official advisories about date-palm sap.

golden_hour: Not acute, but early diagnosis prevents permanent nerve damage and deformity. Any pale, numb skin patch should be checked early - treatment is free and fully curative.
summary: A chronic bacterial infection of skin and nerves caused by Mycobacterium leprae. Bangladesh has controlled but not eliminated leprosy, with pockets in the north. Fully curable with free multidrug therapy; delay causes nerve damage, numbness, and disability.
symptoms: Pale or reddish skin patches with loss of sensation
Numbness in hands, feet, or the patch itself
Painless wounds or burns from loss of sensation
Loss of eyebrows in advanced disease
causes: Infection with Mycobacterium leprae, spread by prolonged close contact via respiratory droplets
risk_factors: Prolonged close household contact with an untreated case
Living in an endemic northern district
complications: Painless ulcers leading to loss of fingers/toes
red_flags: A skin patch that has become numb
New weakness or numbness in a hand or foot
A painless burn or wound the person didn't feel
treatment_overview: Multidrug therapy (MDT) - a combination of dapsone, rifampicin, and clofazimine - is fully curative and provided FREE through government and NGO programs. Early treatment prevents disability. Nerve care, wound care, and physiotherapy prevent deformity.
prevention: Early diagnosis and treatment of cases stops spread
Screening of household contacts
Single-dose rifampicin prophylaxis for close contacts in programs
Reducing stigma so people seek care early
bd_context: Bangladesh's leprosy program (DGHS with NGOs like The Leprosy Mission and Lepra) provides free MDT. Northern districts like Rangpur, Nilphamari, and Thakurgaon report the most cases. Stigma still causes late presentation and disability.

qa.q: Chamrায় sada dag ar obosh - ki kushtho hote pare?
qa.a: Ha, chamrায় fyakashe ba lalche dag ja te onubhuti kome geche (choya na paওয়া) kushtho roger ekta boro lokkhon. Eta obohela na kore doctor ba skin bishesoggo dekhান - kushtho puropuri valo hoy free oshudhe.

qa.q: Is leprosy curable?
qa.a: Yes, completely. Multidrug therapy (MDT) cures leprosy and is given free by the government and NGO programs. Starting early prevents nerve damage and disability. Once treatment starts, the person quickly becomes non-infectious.

qa.q: Kushtho ki chuye chuye chorায়?
qa.a: Kushtho khub sohoje chorায় na. Sudhu dirghodin cikitsa na kora rogir sathe khub ghonishtho basobas korle droplet er madhome chorate pare. Cikitsa shuru hole rogi druto onno der jonno nirapod hoye jay.

qa.q: Where do I get free leprosy treatment in Bangladesh?
qa.a: Through government Upazila Health Complexes and district hospitals, and NGO partners such as The Leprosy Mission and Lepra. MDT medicine is provided free. Northern districts have dedicated leprosy services.

qa.q: Kushtho hole ki ongo baka hoye jay?
qa.a: Deri korle nerve khoti hoye hat baka (claw hand), pa jhule pora (foot drop), ba angul kome jete pare. Kintu age dhora porle o cikitsa nile ei rokom bikriti thekano jay. Tai age i dekhান.

qa.q: Why does leprosy cause numbness?
qa.a: The bacteria attack the nerves in the skin and limbs, so affected areas lose the ability to feel touch, pain, and heat. This is why patients may get painless burns or wounds - and why early treatment to protect the nerves matters.

qa.q: Kushtho roger dag চুলকায় ki?
qa.a: Sadharonoto kushtho er dag চুলকায় na ebong betha o kore na - bostut oi jaygaটা obosh hoye jaওয়া i mul lokkhon. Ei onubhuti-hin dag i eke onno chamra roger theke alada kore.

qa.q: Should family members be checked?
qa.a: Yes. Close household contacts should be screened for early skin patches, and programs may offer a single preventive dose of rifampicin. Early detection in the family stops the spread.

qa.q: Kushtho hole ki biye/kaj kora jabe?
qa.a: Ha. Cikitsa shuru howar por rogi onno der jonno nirapod ebong sadharon jibon japon korte pare - biye, kaj, sob i. Kushtho niye lojja i asol somossa, karon eta deri koray ebong bikriti banায়।

qa.q: Can leprosy affect the eyes?
qa.a: Yes, advanced untreated leprosy can damage the nerves around the eyes and cause blindness. This is another reason early treatment is important. Eye care and protection are part of leprosy management.
